If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-31T20:21:15.6064988Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:20:55.402964248Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57635,\"title\":\"Add CI enforcement to prevent removal of ApiCompatVersion from GA packages\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThere is no CI or build-time enforcement preventing a team from removing `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj` file. If removed, API compatibility checking silently stops running — CI passes with zero errors, and the PR merges without any warning.\\n\\n### Current behavior\\n\\n- `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` automatically **adds** `ApiCompatVersion` when a package transitions from GA to its next prerelease version (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.1.0-beta.1`)\\n- The existing `ValidateRunApiCompat` target (line 16 of `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`) only checks that `RunApiCompat` isn\\u0026#39;t explicitly set to `false`\\n- However, if `ApiCompatVersion` is removed entirely, the `Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat` NuGet package is never referenced, `RunApiCompat` is never set (it\\u0026#39;s empty, not `false`), and the validation check passes silently\\n- The only current defense against removal is PR code review\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nA team could (intentionally or accidentally) delete `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj`, and:\\n1. CI passes ✅\\n2. PR merges ✅  \\n3. Breaking API changes ship undetected ❌\\n\\n### Proposed solution\\n\\nAdd an `ApiCompatRequired` property that is automatically set by `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` when it first adds `ApiCompatVersion` to a GA package:\\n\\n1. **In `Update-PkgVersion.ps1`**: When adding `ApiCompatVersion`, also add `true` to the `.csproj`\\n2. **In `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`**: Add a new validation target:\\n   ```xml\\n   \\n     \\n   \\n   ```\\n3. **Backfill**: Add `true` to all ~292 existing `.csproj` files that currently have `ApiCompatVersion`\\n\\n### Why not a list file?\\n\\nA central list (e.g., `eng/ApiCompatRequired.txt`) was considered but rejected because it requires ongoing maintenance. The property-based approach is fully automatic — `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` manages both properties, and no separate list needs to be kept in sync.\\n\\n### Why pre-GA packages don\\u0026#39;t have ApiCompatVersion\\n\\nPackages that have never shipped a GA release (e.g., still at `1.0.0-beta.3`) don\\u0026#39;t have `ApiCompatVersion` because there\\u0026#39;s no prior GA version to compare against. The `ApiCompatRequired` property distinguishes \\u0026#34;never been GA\\u0026#34; (no property) from \\u0026#34;was GA but someone removed ApiCompatVersion\\u0026#34; (property is `true` but version is empty).\\n\\n### Related\\n\\n- #55311 — Centralize ApiCompat baseline management (adds central baselines and blocks `RunApiCompatForSrc=false` bypass)\\n- PR #57600 — Implementation of #55311\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57635\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:18:30Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:19:17Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-31T20:21:15.6077097Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:20:55.414603577Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57635,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-31T20:21:15.6090821Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:20:55.577364575Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57635,\"title\":\"Add CI enforcement to prevent removal of ApiCompatVersion from GA packages\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThere is no CI or build-time enforcement preventing a team from removing `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj` file. If removed, API compatibility checking silently stops running — CI passes with zero errors, and the PR merges without any warning.\\n\\n### Current behavior\\n\\n- `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` automatically **adds** `ApiCompatVersion` when a package transitions from GA to its next prerelease version (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.1.0-beta.1`)\\n- The existing `ValidateRunApiCompat` target (line 16 of `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`) only checks that `RunApiCompat` isn\\u0026#39;t explicitly set to `false`\\n- However, if `ApiCompatVersion` is removed entirely, the `Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat` NuGet package is never referenced, `RunApiCompat` is never set (it\\u0026#39;s empty, not `false`), and the validation check passes silently\\n- The only current defense against removal is PR code review\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nA team could (intentionally or accidentally) delete `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj`, and:\\n1. CI passes ✅\\n2. PR merges ✅  \\n3. Breaking API changes ship undetected ❌\\n\\n### Proposed solution\\n\\nAdd an `ApiCompatRequired` property that is automatically set by `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` when it first adds `ApiCompatVersion` to a GA package:\\n\\n1. **In `Update-PkgVersion.ps1`**: When adding `ApiCompatVersion`, also add `true` to the `.csproj`\\n2. **In `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`**: Add a new validation target:\\n   ```xml\\n   \\n     \\n   \\n   ```\\n3. **Backfill**: Add `true` to all ~292 existing `.csproj` files that currently have `ApiCompatVersion`\\n\\n### Why not a list file?\\n\\nA central list (e.g., `eng/ApiCompatRequired.txt`) was considered but rejected because it requires ongoing maintenance. The property-based approach is fully automatic — `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` manages both properties, and no separate list needs to be kept in sync.\\n\\n### Why pre-GA packages don\\u0026#39;t have ApiCompatVersion\\n\\nPackages that have never shipped a GA release (e.g., still at `1.0.0-beta.3`) don\\u0026#39;t have `ApiCompatVersion` because there\\u0026#39;s no prior GA version to compare against. The `ApiCompatRequired` property distinguishes \\u0026#34;never been GA\\u0026#34; (no property) from \\u0026#34;was GA but someone removed ApiCompatVersion\\u0026#34; (property is `true` but version is empty).\\n\\n### Related\\n\\n- #55311 — Centralize ApiCompat baseline management (adds central baselines and blocks `RunApiCompatForSrc=false` bypass)\\n- PR #57600 — Implementation of #55311\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57635\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:18:30Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:19:17Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-31T20:21:15.6131686Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:20:59.151088603Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57635,\"title\":\"Add CI enforcement to prevent removal of ApiCompatVersion from GA packages\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThere is no CI or build-time enforcement preventing a team from removing `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj` file. If removed, API compatibility checking silently stops running — CI passes with zero errors, and the PR merges without any warning.\\n\\n### Current behavior\\n\\n- `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` automatically **adds** `ApiCompatVersion` when a package transitions from GA to its next prerelease version (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.1.0-beta.1`)\\n- The existing `ValidateRunApiCompat` target (line 16 of `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`) only checks that `RunApiCompat` isn\\u0026#39;t explicitly set to `false`\\n- However, if `ApiCompatVersion` is removed entirely, the `Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat` NuGet package is never referenced, `RunApiCompat` is never set (it\\u0026#39;s empty, not `false`), and the validation check passes silently\\n- The only current defense against removal is PR code review\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nA team could (intentionally or accidentally) delete `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj`, and:\\n1. CI passes ✅\\n2. PR merges ✅  \\n3. Breaking API changes ship undetected ❌\\n\\n### Proposed solution\\n\\nAdd an `ApiCompatRequired` property that is automatically set by `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` when it first adds `ApiCompatVersion` to a GA package:\\n\\n1. **In `Update-PkgVersion.ps1`**: When adding `ApiCompatVersion`, also add `true` to the `.csproj`\\n2. **In `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`**: Add a new validation target:\\n   ```xml\\n   \\n     \\n   \\n   ```\\n3. **Backfill**: Add `true` to all ~292 existing `.csproj` files that currently have `ApiCompatVersion`\\n\\n### Why not a list file?\\n\\nA central list (e.g., `eng/ApiCompatRequired.txt`) was considered but rejected because it requires ongoing maintenance. The property-based approach is fully automatic — `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` manages both properties, and no separate list needs to be kept in sync.\\n\\n### Why pre-GA packages don\\u0026#39;t have ApiCompatVersion\\n\\nPackages that have never shipped a GA release (e.g., still at `1.0.0-beta.3`) don\\u0026#39;t have `ApiCompatVersion` because there\\u0026#39;s no prior GA version to compare against. The `ApiCompatRequired` property distinguishes \\u0026#34;never been GA\\u0026#34; (no property) from \\u0026#34;was GA but someone removed ApiCompatVersion\\u0026#34; (property is `true` but version is empty).\\n\\n### Related\\n\\n- #55311 — Centralize ApiCompat baseline management (adds central baselines and blocks `RunApiCompatForSrc=false` bypass)\\n- PR #57600 — Implementation of #55311\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57635\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:18:30Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:19:17Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

If removed, API compatibility checking silently stops running — CI passes with zero errors, and the PR merges without any warning.\\n\\n### Current behavior\\n\\n- `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` automatically **adds** `ApiCompatVersion` when a package transitions from GA to its next prerelease version (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.1.0-beta.1`)\\n- The existing `ValidateRunApiCompat` target (line 16 of `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`) only checks that `RunApiCompat` isn\\u0026#39;t explicitly set to `false`\\n- However, if `ApiCompatVersion` is removed entirely, the `Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat` NuGet package is never referenced, `RunApiCompat` is never set (it\\u0026#39;s empty, not `false`), and the validation check passes silently\\n- The only current defense against removal is PR code review\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nA team could (intentionally or accidentally) delete `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj`, and:\\n1. CI passes ✅\\n2. PR merges ✅  \\n3. Breaking API changes ship undetected ❌\\n\\n### Proposed solution\\n\\nAdd an `ApiCompatRequired` property that is automatically set by `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` when it first adds `ApiCompatVersion` to a GA package:\\n\\n1. **In `Update-PkgVersion.ps1`**: When adding `ApiCompatVersion`, also add `true` to the `.csproj`\\n2. **In `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`**: Add a new validation target:\\n   ```xml\\n   \\n     \\n   \\n   ```\\n3. **Backfill**: Add `true` to all ~292 existing `.csproj` files that currently have `ApiCompatVersion`\\n\\n### Why not a list file?\\n\\nA central list (e.g., `eng/ApiCompatRequired.txt`) was considered but rejected because it requires ongoing maintenance. The property-based approach is fully automatic — `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` manages both properties, and no separate list needs to be kept in sync.\\n\\n### Why pre-GA packages don\\u0026#39;t have ApiCompatVersion\\n\\nPackages that have never shipped a GA release (e.g., still at `1.0.0-beta.3`) don\\u0026#39;t have `ApiCompatVersion` because there\\u0026#39;s no prior GA version to compare against. The `ApiCompatRequired` property distinguishes \\u0026#34;never been GA\\u0026#34; (no property) from \\u0026#34;was GA but someone removed ApiCompatVersion\\u0026#34; (property is `true` but version is empty).\\n\\n### Related\\n\\n- #55311 — Centralize ApiCompat baseline management (adds central baselines and blocks `RunApiCompatForSrc=false` bypass)\\n- PR #57600 — Implementation of #55311\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57635\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-redding\",\"id\":66138537,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-redding\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/66138537?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:18:30Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-31T20:19:17Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-31T20:21:15.6184237Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:21:02.937994572Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"query":"repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net is:issue 57635"},"name":"search_issues"}}}
2026-03-31T20:21:15.6207644Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-31T20:21:03.155866588Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4182083528,\"number\":57635,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"Add CI enforcement to prevent removal of ApiCompatVersion from GA packages\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThere is no CI or build-time enforcement preventing a team from removing `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj` file. If removed, API compatibility checking silently stops running — CI passes with zero errors, and the PR merges without any warning.\\n\\n### Current behavior\\n\\n- `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` automatically **adds** `ApiCompatVersion` when a package transitions from GA to its next prerelease version (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.1.0-beta.1`)\\n- The existing `ValidateRunApiCompat` target (line 16 of `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`) only checks that `RunApiCompat` isn't explicitly set to `false`\\n- However, if `ApiCompatVersion` is removed entirely, the `Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompat` NuGet package is never referenced, `RunApiCompat` is never set (it's empty, not `false`), and the validation check passes silently\\n- The only current defense against removal is PR code review\\n\\n### Why this matters\\n\\nA team could (intentionally or accidentally) delete `ApiCompatVersion` from their `.csproj`, and:\\n1. CI passes ✅\\n2. PR merges ✅  \\n3. Breaking API changes ship undetected ❌\\n\\n### Proposed solution\\n\\nAdd an `ApiCompatRequired` property that is automatically set by `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` when it first adds `ApiCompatVersion` to a GA package:\\n\\n1. **In `Update-PkgVersion.ps1`**: When adding `ApiCompatVersion`, also add `\\u003cApiCompatRequired\\u003etrue\\u003c/ApiCompatRequired\\u003e` to the `.csproj`\\n2. **In `eng/Directory.Build.Common.targets`**: Add a new validation target:\\n   ```xml\\n   \\u003cTarget Name=\\\"ValidateApiCompatVersionRequired\\\" BeforeTargets=\\\"Build\\\" \\n           Condition=\\\"'$(ValidateRunApiCompat)' == 'true' and '$(ApiCompatRequired)' == 'true' and '$(ApiCompatVersion)' == '' and '$(IncludeBuildOutput)' != 'false'\\\"\\u003e\\n     \\u003cError Text=\\\"Package $(PackageId) requires ApiCompatVersion to be set. Do not remove ApiCompatVersion from GA packages.\\\" /\\u003e\\n   \\u003c/Target\\u003e\\n   ```\\n3. **Backfill**: Add `\\u003cApiCompatRequired\\u003etrue\\u003c/ApiCompatRequired\\u003e` to all ~292 existing `.csproj` files that currently have `ApiCompatVersion`\\n\\n### Why not a list file?\\n\\nA central list (e.g., `eng/ApiCompatRequired.txt`) was considered but rejected because it requires ongoing maintenance. The property-based approach is fully automatic — `Update-PkgVersion.ps1` manages both properties, and no separate list needs to be kept in sync.\\n\\n### Why pre-GA packages don't have ApiCompatVersion\\n\\nPackages that have never shipped a GA release (e.g., still at `1.0.0-beta.3`) don't have `ApiCompatVersion` because there's no prior GA version to compare against. The `ApiCompatRequired` property distinguishes \\\"never been GA\\\" (no property) from \\\"was GA but someone removed ApiCompatVersion\\\" (property is `true` but version is empty).\\n\\n### Related\\n\\n- #55311 — Centralize ApiCompat baseline management (adds central baselines and blocks `RunApiCompatForSrc=false` bypass)\\n- PR #57600 — Implementation of
